---
title: "Traits and AquaX Sample"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
    message: false
    warning: false
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(terra)
```

```{r}
# Not on server
load('data/META_02122025.Rdata')
```

## Build species information

Associate SDM files with the metadata for the 564 sampled species
```{r}
spp_fs <- list.files('/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/SDM',
                     full.names = TRUE)

df <- data.frame(f = spp_fs) %>%
    mutate(fsize = file.size(f),
           aphiaid = str_extract(basename(f), '[0-9]+') %>% as.integer()) %>%
    left_join(META, by = c('aphiaid' = 'AphiaID'))
```

## Inspect a test species

Identify the structure of the SDM .Rdata for a single species

```{r}
# need to figure out this issue 
load('/home/shares/usgs-cap-rangeshifts/data_aquax/2026_OHARA_TST_MORPHO/SDM/FINAL_EMSDM_EMMEAN_SP_550138.Rdata')
```



```{r}
set.seed(123)
test_spp <- slice_sample(df, n = 1)

load(test_spp$f)

summary(FINALEMMEAN)

r <- rast(FINALEMMEAN)
names(r)
plot(r$Current)
plot(r$Current_NR)
plot(r$RCP85_2100)
```

```{r}
# Assign historical, future, (x-y will be pixel id), and change between historical and future 
finalemmean_test <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = test_spp$aphiaid,
         xyid = paste(x, y, sep = "_")) %>% 
  select(x, y, xyid, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future)

```

```{r}
r_pa <- rast(finalemmean_test)
plot(r_pa$historical)
plot(r_pa$future)
plot(r_pa$pxl_change)

```
```{r}
# Invasion and extinction 
finalemmean_inv_ext <- finalemmean_test %>%
  mutate(inv_ext = case_when(historical == 0 & future == 0 ~ 0, #absence
                             historical == 1 & future == 1 ~ 1, #presence
                             historical == 0 & future == 1 ~ 2, #invasion
                             historical == 1 & future == 0 ~ 3,)) #extinction
```

# Test a bigger subsample

```{r}
set.seed(123)
test_spp_30 <- slice_sample(df, n = 30)
files_30 <- test_spp_30$f
aphiaid_30 <- test_spp_30$aphiaid
#load(test_spp_30$f)

```

```{r}
test_1 <- test_spp_30 %>% 
  filter(aphiaid == aphiaid_30[18]) 

load(test_1$f)
```


```{r}
extract_change <- function(id, df) {
  spp_filter <- df %>% 
    filter(aphiaid == id) 
  
  load(spp_filter$f)
  
  spp_change <- FINALEMMEAN %>% 
  mutate(aphiaid = id) %>% 
  select(x, y, aphiaid, Current, RCP85_2050, cutoff) %>% 
  mutate(historical = case_when(Current < cutoff ~ 0,
                                TRUE ~ 1),
         future = case_when(RCP85_2050 < cutoff ~ 0,
                             TRUE ~ 1)) %>%
  # historical column plus 2 * future gives 0 = absence, 1 = extinction, 2 = invasion, 3 = presence
  mutate(pxl_change = historical + 2 * future,
         remain = case_when(pxl_change == 3 ~ 1,
                            TRUE ~ 0))
  
  r_spp_change <- rast(spp_change)
  
  # return just the pixel change field
  return(r_spp_change[["pxl_change"]])
}
```

```{r}
change_rast <- lapply(aphiaid_30, FUN = extract_change, df = test_spp_30)
```

```{r}
x <- change_rast[[8]]
y <- change_rast[[11]]
```

```{r}
plot(x$pxl_change)
plot(y$pxl_change)
```

```{r}
freq(x$pxl_change)
freq(x$pxl_change, value = 3)
```


## Next, extract the rasters into a dataframe in order to count 
Or stick to raster math (could mask everything but 3s and count those up (the number of species in each cell that remain the same); then count up historical = 1 (historical species) and then could up future = 1 (future species) --> use these to calculate SSI (2 * same / historical + future) 
- challenge - rasters aren't the same size, but cells overlap 
- creating dataframe might create same number of rows

```{r}
raster_x_df <- data.frame(values(x)) #%>% 
  #mutate(pixel_id = row_number())
```

```{r}
raster_y_df <- data.frame(values(y)) #%>% 
  #mutate(pixel_id = row_number())
```

```{r}
# The rasters are not the same extent (different cropping based on species), so I need to union them to find the largest area and then pad the individual rasters
change_collection <- sprc(change_rast)
max_extent <- ext(change_collection)
```

```{r}
align_list <- function(r) {
  extend(r, max_extent)
}
```

```{r}
change_rast_aligned <- lapply(change_rast, align_list)
```

```{r}
change_stack <- rast(change_rast_aligned)
```

```{r}
# Count the number of "3s" - shared species
# adding row name? 3 works, since it's unique, but otherwise I ned to filter these layers before the stack 
spp_same_count <- sum(change_stack == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_ext <- sum(change_stack == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
spp_inv <- sum(change_stack == 2, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
spp_ssi <- (2*spp_same_count)/((2*spp_same_count) + spp_ext + spp_inv)
```

```{r}
#Species richness - historic
plot(spp_same_count + spp_ext)
```

```{r}
#Species richness - future
plot(spp_same_count + spp_inv)
```


```{r}
# Species SSI
plot(spp_ssi)
```

## Try running in parallel with app function 

```{r}
count_change <- function(x){
  sum(x == 3, na.rm = TRUE)
}
```

```{r}
system.time({
  spp_same_count_pl <- app(change_stack, count_change, cores = 5)
  })

```

```{r}
plot(spp_same_count_pl)
```



```{r}
library(parallel)
```

#doesn't work with SpatRasters
```{r}
system.time({
  out_list <- parallel::mclapply(aphiaid_30, FUN = extract_change, df = test_spp_30, mc.cores = 3)
})
```
